what: Simulate and fit a generalized linear mixed model
title: Lanczos for penalized likelihood models
text: first simulate data compound lognormal-Gamma process: define function compute joint likelihood, also allowing us calculate outputs later use: Finally, fit model log-linked generalized linear mixed model (GLMM):
code: set.seed(123) n = 30 n_sum = 3  # log-linked normal deviates u = 0 + rnorm(n)  # Conditional gamma process y = rgamma( n, shape = 1/0.5^2, scale = exp(u) * 0.5^2 ) nll = function(p){   sumexpu = sum(exp(p$u[seq_len(n_sum)]))   ADREPORT( sumexpu )   REPORT( sumexpu )   nll1 = dnorm(p$u, mean=p$mu, sd=exp(p$logsd), log=TRUE)   nll2 = dgamma(      y,      shape = 1/exp(2*p$logcv),      scale = exp(p$u) * exp(2*p$logcv),      log=TRUE   )   jnll = -1 * ( sum(nll1) + sum(nll2) )   if(what == "jnll") return(jnll)   if(what == "sumexpu") return(sumexpu)   if(what == "biascorr") return(jnll + p$eps * sumexpu) }  # Starting list of parameters parlist = list(u=u*0, mu = 0, logsd = 0, logcv = 0, eps = 0) # Build RTMB object what = "jnll" obj = RTMB::MakeADFun(    nll,    parlist,   map = list(eps = factor(NA)),   random = "u",   silent = TRUE )  # optimize  opt = nlminb( obj$par, obj$fn, obj$gr ) opt #> $par #>         mu      logsd      logcv  #> -0.1052225 -0.1326400 -0.5984998  #>  #> $objective #> [1] 36.46907 #>  #> $convergence #> [1] 0 #>  #> $iterations #> [1] 13 #>  #> $evaluations #> function gradient  #>       17       14  #>  #> $message #> [1] "relative convergence (4)"

what: Fit as marginal likelihood without Cholesky decomposition
title: Lanczos for penalized likelihood models
text: first show can re-fit model using marginal likelihood, evaluated using Hutchinson-Lanczos instead Cholesky factorization default TMB:
code: parlist$eps = numeric() pen0 = lanczos_MakeADFun(   nll,    parlist,   random = "u",   k = 10,   silent = TRUE )  # optimize  opt_pen0 = nlminb( pen0$par, pen0$fn ) opt_pen0 #> $par #>         mu      logsd      logcv  #> -0.1084795 -0.1284993 -0.6074894  #>  #> $objective #> [1] 36.46912 #>  #> $convergence #> [1] 1 #>  #> $iterations #> [1] 11 #>  #> $evaluations #> function gradient  #>       31       35  #>  #> $message #> [1] "false convergence (8)"

what: Fit as a penalized likelihood model
title: Lanczos for penalized likelihood models
text: Next, show model can refitted using penalized likelihood, conditional upon fixed values variance parameters: Alternatively, provide Newton optimizer using truncated conjugate gradient, operates using Hessian-vector products without ever forming full Hessian matrix: can use stochastic trace estimation Lanczos method approximate log-determinant Hessian random effects: Using log-determinant, can also compare log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos full calculation:
code: # Define RTMB object for penalized likelihood newmap = list(   mu = factor(NA),    logsd = factor(NA),    logcv = factor(NA),   eps = factor(NA) ) pen = RTMB::MakeADFun(    nll,    obj$env$parList(),    map = newmap,   silent = TRUE )  # Re-optimize opt_pen = nlminb( pen$par, pen$fn, pen$gr ) # Extract tape and make Hessian-vector-product function tape = GetTape(pen) Hq = make_Hq( tape, pen$par )  # run Newton optimizer opt_pen2 = newton_CG(   par = pen$par,   fn = tape,   gr = tape$jacfun(),   Hq = Hq ) #> value: 41.78943 mgc: 9.836576e-13 ustep: 0.9999 # log-determinant using Lanczos Hq = make_Hq( GetTape(pen), opt_pen$par ) lanczos_logdet( Hq, k = 10, m = 3 ) #> [1] 44.4956 44.4956 44.4956  # log-determinant for marginal likelihood H = obj$env$spHess(par = obj$env$last.par.best, random = TRUE) Matrix::determinant( H )$modulus #> [1] 44.4956 #> attr(,"logarithm") #> [1] TRUE # Marginal likelihoods using Lanczos lanczos_nll( pen, k = 10, m = 10 ) #>      nll   sd_nll  #> 36.46907  0.00000  # Marginal likelihood using Laplace opt$obj #> [1] 36.46907

title: Lanczos for penalized likelihood models
text: Alternatively, can apply Lanczos methods standard errors parameters. , sample first three random effects Alternatively, can apply Lanczos methods approximate Hessian respect derived quantity. involves calculating gradient derived quantity respect coefficients: can use gradient approximate samples Hessian matrix use calculate Monte Carlo estimator standard errors: Alternatively, can calculate standard error derived quantity using Lanczos within delta method:
code: samples = lanczos_sample(    Hq = Hq,    q = c( rep(1,3), rep(0,n-3) ),    k = 30,    n = 1000,    orthogonalize = TRUE )  # sdrep = sdreport( obj, ignore.parm.uncertainty = TRUE ) as.list(sdrep, what = "Std. Error")$u[1:3] #> [1] 0.4839019 0.4864751 0.4066186 apply( samples, MARGIN = 1, FUN = sd)[1:3] #> [1] 0.4782002 0.4887318 0.4051458 what = "sumexpu" grad = RTMB::MakeADFun(    nll,    obj$env$parList(),    map = newmap,   silent = TRUE )$gr( pen$par )[1,] samples = lanczos_sample(    Hq = Hq,    q = grad,    k = 30,    n = 1000,    orthogonalize = TRUE )  # Compare SD of samples with the delta method sumexpu1 = apply( samples, MARGIN = 2, FUN = \(v)pen$report(v)$sumexpu ) c( pen$report()$sumexpu, sd(sumexpu1) ) #> [1] 4.0641214 0.9435151 summary(sdrep)['sumexpu',] #>   Estimate Std. Error  #>   4.064121   1.194484 #  Var = lanczos_variance(   Hq = Hq,   q = grad,   k = 3 )  # Compare with the delta method c( pen$report()$sumexpu, sqrt(Var) ) #> [1] 4.064121 1.194484 summary(sdrep)['sumexpu',] #>   Estimate Std. Error  #>   4.064121   1.194484

title: Lanczos for penalized likelihood models
text: Finally, can use gradient log-likelihood respect dummy variable epsilon correct retransformation bias: Runtime vignette: 4.83 secs
code: # One-sided finite difference what = "biascorr" phat = obj$env$parList() phat$eps = 0.0001 pen_hi = RTMB::MakeADFun(    nll,    parameters = phat,    map = newmap,   silent = TRUE )  # Re-optimize opt_hi = optim(    pen_hi$par, pen_hi$fn, pen_hi$gr,    method = "L-BFGS-B", control = list(factr = 1e-2)  )  # Calculate marginal nll for finite-difference nll_hi = lanczos_nll( pen_hi, k = 10, m = 10, seed = 123 ) nll_mid = lanczos_nll( pen, k = 10, m = 10, seed = 123 )  # Epsilon bias-correction estimator sdrep = sdreport( obj, bias.correct = TRUE )  # (nll_hi['nll'] - nll_mid['nll']) / (phat$eps) #>      nll  #> 4.733918 summary(sdrep)['sumexpu',] #>            Estimate          Std. Error Est. (bias.correct) Std. (bias.correct)  #>            4.064121            1.625808            4.734025                  NA

title: Lanczos methods for mixed-effects models in RTMB
text: Methods using Krylov subspace recursion RTMB, use matrix-vector products replace sparse-Cholesky factorization.

title: Lanczos methods for mixed-effects models in RTMB
text: lanczosRTMB designed approximate properties hierarchical model implemented RTMB without ever constructing inverting matrix second derivatives (“Hessian matrix”). can used approximate: Standard errors using matrix-free delta method log-determinant Hessian using stochastic trace estimation log marginal likelihood using Laplace approximation log-density GMRF Iterative Newton solvers involving truncated conjugate gradients

title: Lanczos methods for mixed-effects models in RTMB
text: ’s many avenues improvement. include: Improving quasi-Newton inner optimizer using Hq, .e., variant conjugate gradient; Moving FD logic obj$gr tape; Exploring conjugate gradient algorithm implicit solution gradient log-determinant;

what: Truncated conjugate gradient using Hessian-vector products — CG
title: Truncated conjugate gradient using Hessian-vector products — CG
text: Approximate soution linear system \(Hq = b\) using truncated conjugate gradient, without forming Hessian matrix directly instead using Hessian-vector products e.g., using forwards--reverse autodiff Adapted mcmcsae::CG GPL-3 licence use Hessian-vector products

title: Truncated conjugate gradient using Hessian-vector products — CG
code: CG(   b,   Hq,   x = 0 * b,   Minv = Diagonal(n = length(b)),   max.it = length(b),   e = 1e-10,   stop_if_nonPD = TRUE,   silent = TRUE )

title: Truncated conjugate gradient using Hessian-vector products — CG
text: b vector solve Hq efficient Hessian-vector product function, e.g., make_Hq x initial guess solution Minv preconditioner matrix (uses Identity default) max.maximum iterations (often less length(b)) e error criterion stop_if_nonPD whether stop CG recusion positive definite silent silent print progress?

title: Truncated conjugate gradient using Hessian-vector products — CG
code: library(Matrix)  # Settings n = 10^4 rho = 0.99  # Simulate AR1 process approaching random walk (i.e., ill-conditioned inner problem) P = bandSparse( n = n, k = c(-1), diagonals = list(rep(1,n)) ) Q = (Diagonal(n) - rho * t(P)) %*% (Diagonal(n) - rho * P) x = RTMB:::rgmrf0( n= 1, Q = Q )[,1] y = x + 0.1 * rnorm(n) which_seen = sample( seq_len(n), size = n/10, replace = FALSE) y[-which_seen] = NA  nll = function(p){   -dgmrf(p$x, Q = Q, log = TRUE) - sum(dnorm(y, p$x, sd = 0.1, log=TRUE), na.rm=TRUE) } parlist = list( x=rnorm(n) )  tape = MakeTape(nll, parlist) gr = tape$jacfun() Hq = make_Hq( tape, x = unlist(parlist) ) H = gr$jacfun(sparse = TRUE)  # Test unregularized solution b = gr(unlist(parlist))[1,] Hess = H(unlist(parlist)) x = solve( Hess, b) out = CG(   b = b,   Hq = Hq ) plot( x, out$x )   # Test trust-region regularization Hess2 = Hess + 2 * Diagonal(n=n) x2 = solve( Hess2, b) out = CG(   b = b,   Hq = \(b) Hq(b) + 2*b ) plot( x, out$x )

title: Calculate Lanczos approximation — lanczos
text: Calculate Q, alpha beta Lanzos approximation

title: Calculate Lanczos approximation — lanczos
code: lanczos(Hq, q, k, x = attr(Hq, "env")$x0, orthogonalize = FALSE, tol = 1e-12)

title: Calculate Lanczos approximation — lanczos
text: Hq function calculates product H %*% q given probe q parameters x q initial vector used defining Kyrlov subspace k dimension Kyrlov subspace x parameter vector used calculating Hessian matrix orthogonalize Whether two-pass Gram-Schmidt re-normalization (much slower) tol numerical tolerance stopping algorithm given terms identifiable

title: Calculate Lanczos approximation — lanczos
code: H = diag(exp(rnorm(5))) q = rep(1,5) Hq = function(q, x) (H %*% q)[,1]  L = lanczos(Hq, x = NULL, q, k = 5, ortho = TRUE) T = tridiag(L$alpha, L$beta)  # Should match H if and only if L$m = nrow(H) range(L$Q %*% T %*% t(L$Q) - H) #> [1] -3.341163e-16  2.220446e-16

what: Approximate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method (EXPERIMENTAL) — lanczos_MakeADFun
title: Approximate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method (EXPERIMENTAL) — lanczos_MakeADFun
text: Make function returns Lanczos-Laplace approximation user-supplied joint likelihood designated random effects

title: Approximate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method (EXPERIMENTAL) — lanczos_MakeADFun
code: lanczos_MakeADFun(   func,   parameters,   random,   k,   profile = NULL,   m = 3,   method = "newton_CG",   seed = 123,   make_gr = TRUE,   silent = TRUE )

title: Approximate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method (EXPERIMENTAL) — lanczos_MakeADFun
text: func Function taking parameter list (parameter vector) input. parameters Parameter list (parameter vector) used func. random Character vector defining random effect parameters. See also regexp. k dimension Kyrlov subspace profile Parameters profile likelihood (subset appended random Laplace approximation disabled). m number probe-vectors use approximating average standard deviation log-determinant method whether use newton_CG gradient-based low-memory option specifically "L-BFGS-B" optim optimize inner problem seed NULL, sets seed.  helfpul given Hutchinson probe vectors randomly sampled, comparisons lower variance using fixed seed. make_gr whether make approximated gradient using fixed probes (slow large models) silent Disable tracing information?

title: Approximate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method (EXPERIMENTAL) — lanczos_MakeADFun
text: object (list) class tinyVAST. Elements include: par parameter-vector fixed effects fn function returns Lanczos-Laplace approximation given fixed effects env environment local variables Hq function returns Hessian-vector product (use debugging)

title: Approximate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method (EXPERIMENTAL) — lanczos_MakeADFun
text: gradient uses finite-difference applied fixed set probes, inspired Dong et al. (2017) stochasticLQ option GPyTorch.

title: Approximate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method (EXPERIMENTAL) — lanczos_MakeADFun
text: Dong, K., Eriksson, D., Nickisch, H., Bindel, D., & Wilson, . G. (2017). Scalable log determinants Gaussian process kernel learning. Advances Neural Information Processing Systems, 30. https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper/2017/hash/976abf49974d4686f87192efa0513ae0-Abstract.html

title: Approximate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method (EXPERIMENTAL) — lanczos_MakeADFun
code: # Simulate lognormal-gamma process set.seed(123) library(RTMB) n = 30 n_sum = 3 u = 0 + rnorm(n) y = rgamma( n, shape = 1/0.5^2, scale = exp(u) * 0.5^2 )  # Fit as GLMM what = "jnll" nll = function(p){   sumexpu = sum(exp(p$u[seq_len(n_sum)]))   nll1 = dnorm(p$u, mean=p$mu, sd=exp(p$logsd), log=TRUE)   nll2 = dgamma(y, shape = 1/exp(2*p$logcv), scale = exp(p$u) * exp(2*p$logcv), log=TRUE)   jnll = -1 * ( sum(nll1) + sum(nll2) )   return(jnll) }  # Build obj = lanczos_MakeADFun( nll, list(u=u, mu = 0, logsd = 0, logcv = 0), random = "u", k = 10 ) opt = nlminb( obj$par, obj$fn )  # Compare with RTMB obj2 = MakeADFun( nll, list(u=u, mu = 0, logsd = 0, logcv = 0), random = "u", silent = TRUE ) opt2 = nlminb( obj2$par, obj2$fn, obj2$gr ) opt$par - opt2$par #>         mu      logsd      logcv  #> -0.1585939  0.1860163 -5.7699826   # Fit again using FD gradient for Lanczos method using fixed probe-recursion   # This requires an optimizer that is tolerant to small imprecision in the gradient   # And it ends at a slightly different estimator opt3 = optim( obj$par, obj$fn, obj$gr, method = "BFGS" ) opt3$par - opt2$par #>           mu        logsd        logcv  #>  0.003816543 -0.005030572  0.010297195

title: Approximate the log-density of a GMRF using Lanczos — lanczos_dgmrf
text: x parameter vector used calculating Hessian matrix Q Sparse precision matrix k dimension Kyrlov subspace mu Mean parameter vector m number probe-vectors use approximating average standard deviation log-determinant log Logical; Return log density? seed NULL, sets seed.  helfpul given Hutchinson probe vectors randomly sampled, comparisons lower variance using fixed seed. orthogonalize Whether two-pass Gram-Schmidt re-normalization (much slower)

title: Approximate the log-density of a GMRF using Lanczos — lanczos_dgmrf
code: library(RTMB) library(Matrix) library(lanczosRTMB)  # Create a precision for a 1D AR1 process P = bandSparse( n = 100, k = c(-1,1), diagonals = list(rep(0.5,99),rep(0.5,99)) ) Q = (Diagonal(100) - 0.5*t(P) ) %*% (Diagonal(100) - 0.5*P) x = RTMB:::rgmrf0(n = 1, Q )[,1]  # Exact density using RTMB dgmrf(x, Q = Q, log = TRUE) #> [1] -141.6156  # Lanczos-approximated density lanczos_dgmrf(x, Q = Q, k = 20, log = TRUE) #> [1] -141.1068 -146.3984 -138.9235

code: lanczos_logdet(   Hq,   k,   m,   x = attr(Hq, "env")$x0,   which_random = attr(Hq, "env")$which_random,   seed = NULL,   orthogonalize = TRUE,   Q_list = NULL,   return_extra = FALSE )

title: Estimate log-determinant likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_logdet
text: Hq function calculates product H %*% q given probe q parameters x k dimension Kyrlov subspace m number probe-vectors use approximating average standard deviation log-determinant x parameter vector used calculating Hessian matrix which_random integer-vector indicating elements x correspond random effects, probe q length length(which_random) seed NULL, sets seed.  helfpul given Hutchinson probe vectors randomly sampled, comparisons lower variance using fixed seed. orthogonalize Whether two-pass Gram-Schmidt re-normalization (much slower) Q_list optional list probes returned prior Lanczos run.  uses fixed probes speed evaluations gradients. return_extra whether return probes internal constructions.

title: Estimate log-determinant likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_logdet
text: model independent random effects, variance stochastic trace estimation zero

title: Estimate log-determinant likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_logdet
code: # Simulate lognormal-gamma process set.seed(123) library(RTMB) n = 30 n_sum = 3 u = 0 + rnorm(n) y = rgamma( n, shape = 1/0.5^2, scale = exp(u) * 0.5^2 )  # Fit as GLMM what = "jnll" nll = function(p){   nll1 = dnorm(p$u, mean=p$mu, sd=exp(p$logsd), log=TRUE)   nll2 = dgamma(y, shape = 1/exp(2*p$logcv), scale = exp(p$u) * exp(2*p$logcv), log=TRUE)   jnll = -1 * ( sum(nll1) + sum(nll2) )   return(jnll) } params = list(u=u, mu = 0, logsd = 0, logcv = 0)  # Make RTMB object obj = RTMB::MakeADFun( nll, params, random = "u", silent = TRUE )  # Make Lanczos object tape = MakeTape( nll, params ) Hq = make_Hq( tape, unlist(params), which_random = 1:30 )  # Compare determinant at start values lanczos_logdet( Hq, k = 10, m = 3 ) #> [1] 18.43706 18.43706 18.43706 H = obj$env$spHess(par = obj$env$par, random = TRUE) sum(log(eigen(H)$values)) #> [1] 18.43706  # Compare determinant at new values x_new = unlist(params)   x_new['logsd'] = 1 lanczos_logdet( Hq, x = x_new, k = 10, m = 3 ) #> [1] -1.454869 -1.454869 -1.454869 H = obj$env$spHess(par = x_new, random = TRUE) sum(log(eigen(H)$values)) #> [1] -1.454869

title: Estimate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_nll
text: function intended integrating across parameters, e.g., supplying penalized likelihood model fixed effects mapped prior estimate.  control parameters estimate, use lanczos_MakeADFun

title: Estimate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_nll
code: # Simulate lognormal-gamma process set.seed(123) library(RTMB) n = 30 n_sum = 3 u = 0 + rnorm(n) y = rgamma( n, shape = 1/0.5^2, scale = exp(u) * 0.5^2 )  # Fit as GLMM what = "jnll" nll = function(p){   nll1 = dnorm(p$u, mean=p$mu, sd=exp(p$logsd), log=TRUE)   nll2 = dgamma(y, shape = 1/exp(2*p$logcv), scale = exp(p$u) * exp(2*p$logcv), log=TRUE)   jnll = -1 * ( sum(nll1) + sum(nll2) )   return(jnll) } obj = RTMB::MakeADFun( nll, list(u=u, mu = 0, logsd = 0, logcv = 0), random = "u", silent = TRUE ) opt = nlminb( obj$par, obj$fn, obj$gr ) sdrep = sdreport(obj, bias.correct = TRUE ) H = obj$env$spHess(par = obj$env$last.par.best, random = TRUE)  # Re-do as penalized likelihood newmap = list(mu = factor(NA), logsd = factor(NA), logcv = factor(NA)) pen = RTMB::MakeADFun( nll, obj$env$parList(), map = newmap, silent = TRUE ) opt_pen = nlminb( pen$par, pen$fn, pen$gr )  # Compare marginal likelihoods lanczos_nll( pen, k = 10, m = 10 ) #>      nll   sd_nll  #> 36.46908  0.00000  opt$obj #> [1] 36.46907

title: Sample from Lanczos method — lanczos_sample
text: Hq function calculates product H %*% q given probe q parameters x q initial vector used defining Kyrlov subspace k dimension Kyrlov subspace x parameter vector used calculating Hessian matrix nsamp number samples orthogonalize Whether two-pass Gram-Schmidt re-normalization (much slower)

title: Sample from Lanczos method — lanczos_sample
code: # Simulate lognormal-gamma process set.seed(123) library(RTMB) n = 30 n_sum = 3 u = 0 + rnorm(n) y = rgamma( n, shape = 1/0.5^2, scale = exp(u) * 0.5^2 )  # Fit as GLMM what = "jnll" nll = function(p){   sumexpu = sum(exp(p$u[seq_len(n_sum)]))   ADREPORT( sumexpu )   REPORT( sumexpu )   nll1 = dnorm(p$u, mean=p$mu, sd=exp(p$logsd), log=TRUE)   nll2 = dgamma(y, shape = 1/exp(2*p$logcv), scale = exp(p$u) * exp(2*p$logcv), log=TRUE)   jnll = -1 * ( sum(nll1) + sum(nll2) )   if(what == "jnll") return(jnll)   if(what == "sumexpu") return(sumexpu) } obj = RTMB::MakeADFun( nll, list(u=u, mu = 0, logsd = 0, logcv = 0), random = "u", silent = TRUE ) opt = nlminb( obj$par, obj$fn, obj$gr ) sdrep = sdreport(obj, bias.correct = TRUE ) H = obj$env$spHess(par = obj$env$last.par.best, random = TRUE)  # Re-do as penalized likelihood newmap = list(mu = factor(NA), logsd = factor(NA), logcv = factor(NA)) pen = RTMB::MakeADFun( nll, obj$env$parList(), map = newmap, silent = TRUE ) opt_pen = nlminb( pen$par, pen$fn, pen$gr ) Hq = make_Hq( GetTape(pen), opt_pen$par )  # Gradient-based Lanczos sampling what = "sumexpu" grad = RTMB::MakeADFun( nll, obj$env$parList(), map = newmap, silent = TRUE )$gr( opt_pen$par )[1,] what = "jnll" sample_x = function(n){ x = rnorm(n); return( x / sqrt(sum(x^2))) } samples = lanczos_sample(   Hq = Hq,   q = grad,   k = 30,   n = 1000,   orthogonalize = TRUE )  # Samples from Lanczos for parameters that contribute to derived quantity samples = sweep( samples, MARGIN = 1, FUN = "+", STATS = opt_pen$par ) apply( samples, MARGIN = 1, FUN = sd ) #>  [1] 0.4785608 0.4843596 0.4114760 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 #>  [8] 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 #> [15] 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 #> [22] 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 #> [29] 0.0000000 0.0000000  # Samples from full Hessian should approximately match samp2 = t(mvtnorm::rmvnorm( n = 1000, sigma = as.matrix(solve(H)) )) apply( samp2, MARGIN = 1, FUN = sd ) #>  [1] 0.4909493 0.4696421 0.4120659 0.4423042 0.4643884 0.3977407 0.4334791 #>  [8] 0.5479412 0.5325241 0.4876392 0.4092560 0.4274928 0.4818361 0.4363509 #> [15] 0.5039307 0.3924580 0.4678129 0.6049901 0.4369197 0.5336944 0.5921870 #> [22] 0.4993766 0.5171501 0.4881900 0.5038213 0.5131423 0.4787957 0.4439222 #> [29] 0.5402735 0.4108968  # Compare bias-correction sumexpu_z = apply( samples, MARGIN = 2, FUN = \(x) pen$report(x)$sumexpu ) mean(sumexpu_z) #> [1] 4.473128 summary(sdrep)['sumexpu',] #>            Estimate          Std. Error Est. (bias.correct) Std. (bias.correct)  #>            4.064121            1.625808            4.734025                  NA

what: Estimate variance using Lanczos method — lanczos_variance
title: Estimate variance using Lanczos method — lanczos_variance
text: Estimate squared standard error (variance) parameter derived quantity without forming Hessian matrix directly

title: Estimate variance using Lanczos method — lanczos_variance
code: lanczos_variance(   Hq,   q,   x = attr(Hq, "env")$x0,   k = c(25, 30),   min_spectral_ratio = 1e-10,   orthogonalize = FALSE )

title: Estimate variance using Lanczos method — lanczos_variance
text: Hq function calculates product H %*% q given probe q parameters x q vector use calculating variance, either indicator single parameter, gradient evaluated MLE derived quantity x parameter vector used calculating Hessian matrix k can vector min_spectral_ratio ratio minimum maximum ratio, values < minimum truncated minimum (min_spectral_ratio=0 disables truncation) orthogonalize Whether two-pass Gram-Schmidt re-normalization (much slower)

what: Make function to calculate H %*% q — make_Hq
title: Make function to calculate H %*% q — make_Hq
text: Given TMB object, make function efficiently calculates H %*% q without constructing H , instead using grad_u( grad_u(f) %** q) given function f(x) returns negative log-likelihood given x = u fixed v.  can used e.g. Lanczos methods H large construct explicitly

title: Make function to calculate H %*% q — make_Hq
code: make_Hq(tape, x0, which_random = seq_along(x0))

title: Make function to calculate H %*% q — make_Hq
text: tape Alternative specifying obj x0 parameter vector x (list coersed vector) used default evaluating H which_random integer-vector indicating elements x correspond random effects, probe q length length(which_random)

title: Make function to calculate H %*% q — make_Hq
text: function two arguments: q probe vector length length(which_random) vector length length(x0), Hessian calculated

title: Make function to calculate H %*% q — make_Hq
text: output Hq = make_Hq( tape, x ) takes argument probe \(\mathbf{q}\) outputs \(\mathbf{Hq}\).  change point \(\mathbf{Hq}\) evaluated, assign new value attr(Hq,"env")$x.  RTMB force.update() update tape based new value. qprime defined internally qprime[which_random] = q qprime[!which_random] = 0, length(qprime) equal length(x)

title: Make function to calculate H %*% q — make_Hq
code: # Simulate lognormal-gamma process set.seed(123) library(RTMB) n = 30 n_sum = 3 u = 0 + rnorm(n) y = rgamma( n, shape = 1/0.5^2, scale = exp(u) * 0.5^2 )  # Fit as GLMM nll = function(p){   nll1 = dnorm(p$u, mean=p$mu, sd=exp(p$logsd), log=TRUE)   nll2 = dgamma(y, shape = 1/exp(2*p$logcv), scale = exp(p$u) * exp(2*p$logcv), log=TRUE)   jnll = -1 * ( sum(nll1) + sum(nll2) )   return(jnll) }  # Build with RTMB params = list(u=u, mu = 0, logsd = 0, logcv = 0) obj = MakeADFun( nll, params, random = "u", silent = TRUE )  # Build with Lanczos tape = MakeTape( nll, params ) which_random = 1:30 Hq = make_Hq(   tape,   x = params,   which_random = which_random )  # Compare them q = rnorm(length(which_random)) all.equal(   Hq(q),   (obj$env$spHess(par = unlist(params), random=TRUE) %*% q)[,1] ) #> [1] TRUE  # Compare them when passing new value x_new = unlist(params)   x_new['logsd'] = 1 all.equal(   Hq(q, x_new),   (obj$env$spHess(par = x_new, random=TRUE) %*% q)[,1] ) #> [1] TRUE

what: Nonlinear minimizer using line search with approximated Newton solution — newton_CG
title: Nonlinear minimizer using line search with approximated Newton solution — newton_CG
text: Nonlinear minimizer designed use cheap Hessian-vector products using make_Hq.  minimizer approximates Newton update using truncated conjugate gradient, adapting regularization parameter designed using line-search step.

title: Nonlinear minimizer using line search with approximated Newton solution — newton_CG
code: newton_CG(   par,   fn,   gr,   Hq,   gr_tol = 1e-08,   e_ratio = 0.01,   maxit_newton = 100,   maxit_CG = min(100, length(par)),   c1 = 0.01,   beta = 0.5,   line_steps = 20,   smartsearch = TRUE,   ustep = 1,   power = 0.5,   u0 = 1e-04,   stop_if_nonPD = smartsearch,   tol10 = 0.001,   diagnostics = FALSE,   silent = FALSE )

title: Nonlinear minimizer using line search with approximated Newton solution — newton_CG
text: par initial parameter vector fn function evaluate negative log-likelihood gr function evaluate gradient negative log-likelihood Hq efficient Hessian-vector product function, e.g., make_Hq gr_tol early stopping condition gradient Newton solver e_ratio early stopping condition error CG, relative initial gradient maxit_newton maximum iterations Newton solver maxit_CG maximum iterations CG solution Newton iteration c1 stopping condition line search given CG solution Newton iteration, \(1>c1>0\) seeks prevent overshoot resulting zigzagging. beta updates line search stepsize alpha Armijo sufficient decrease condition fails line_steps number steps explore linear-search given Newton update smartsearch Turn adaptive stepsize algorithm non-convex problems? ustep Adaptive stepsize initial guess 0 1. power Parameter controlling adaptive stepsize. u0 Parameter controlling adaptive stepsize. stop_if_nonPD whether stop CG recusion positive definite tol10 Try exit last 10 iterations improved . diagnostics whether provide extra diagnostics Newton iteration silent silent print progress?

title: Nonlinear minimizer using line search with approximated Newton solution — newton_CG
text:
minimizer approximates Newton steps \(x_{+1} = x_{} - H(x_i)^{-1} g(x_i)\), \(H(x_i) = 
abla^2 f(x_i)\) Hessian matrix \(g(x_i) = 
abla f(x_i)\) gradient negative log-likelihood \(f(x_i)\). uses several strategies numerical computational efficiency. approximates Newton step \(H(x_i)^{-1} g(x_i)\) using truncated conjugate gradient algorithm involves Hessian-vector products \(H(x_i) v\). recursively improves solution desired accuracy reached, controlled gr_tol, without ever constructing Hessian matrix ; approximates Newton solution \(H(x_i)^{-1} g(x_i)\) without ever constructing storing Hessian \(H(x_i)\), instead computing Hessian-vector product \(H(x_i) v = 
abla( v^T 
abla f(x_i) )\) using autodifferentiation RTMB; approximating step direction \(H(x_i)^{-1} g(x_i)\), uses line-search algorithm decreasing step-size needed find suitable decrease objective function, controlled line_steps, beta, c1; CG monitored identify whether Hessian matrix positive definite (PD), PD CG may terminated, controlled stop_if_nonPD. using smartsearch = TRUE, Hessian PD, regularization ustep decreased, corresponding increase \(t= rac{1}{u}-1\) Newton step actually using \((H + tI)^{-1} g\) \(g\) gradient, \(1>u>0\) corresponds \(t>0\). increase decrease ustep (associated decrease/increase \(t\)) copied TMB::newton(). smartsearch = FALSE \(ustep=1\) \(t=0\) CG uses Hessian corresponding unregularized Newton steps. smartsearch behavior controlled u0, ustep, power, tol10, corresponds adaptive "trust-region".
